pr.title: Port TCS3200 sketch from Arduino UNO R4 WiFi to Seeed XIAO ESP32-C3
pr.body:
`tools/uno_r4_wifi_tcs3200_http.ino` used UNO R4 WiFi-specific headers (`WiFiS3.h`, `WiFiSSLClient`) and ArduinoBLE APIs that don't build on ESP32. This adds a new sketch targeting the XIAO ESP32-C3 with the requested default pin mapping, preserving all sensor logic and HTTP behavior.

### Networking
- `WiFiS3.h` → `WiFi.h` + `WiFiClientSecure.h` (bundled with Espressif esp32 board package)
- `WiFiSSLClient` → `WiFiClientSecure` with `setInsecure()` — matches original's no-cert-validation behavior

### BLE
- ArduinoBLE (`BLERead`/`BLENotify`) → ESP32 BLE Arduino (`BLEDevice.h`) with identical service/characteristic UUIDs
- `BLE.poll()` removed from `loop()` — ESP32 BLE stack is interrupt-driven
- Same `#if __has_include` guard for graceful fallback to WiFi-only if BLE headers are absent

### Pin mapping (XIAO ESP32-C3 defaults)
| Signal | Pin |
|--------|-----|
| S0 | D0 (GPIO2) |
| S1 | D1 (GPIO3) |
| S2 | D2 (GPIO4) |
| S3 | D3 (GPIO5) |
| OUT | D4 (GPIO6) |
| LED | D5 (GPIO7) |

### Other
- `DEVICE_ID` updated to `"xiao-esp32-c3"`
- Board package / library requirements noted in the file header
- Original `uno_r4_wifi_tcs3200_http.ino` left untouched

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> Convert `tools/uno_r4_wifi_tcs3200_http.ino` in repository `thecodingmastero/PlaqueTracker_App` from Arduino UNO R4 WiFi to Seeed XIAO ESP32-C3, preserving the existing behavior as much as possible.
> 
> Context:
> - The user wants the existing sketch adapted for a XIAO ESP32-C3 using default pins.
> - Source file path: `tools/uno_r4_wifi_tcs3200_http.ino`
> - Repository: `thecodingmastero/PlaqueTracker_App`
> - Source URL referenced by the user: `https://github.com/thecodingmastero/PlaqueTracker_App/blob/main/tools/uno_r4_wifi_tcs3200_http.ino`
> - The current sketch uses `WiFiS3.h` and `WiFiSSLClient`, which are UNO R4 WiFi-specific and should be replaced with ESP32-compatible networking.
> 
> Requirements:
> 1. Update the sketch so it builds for Seeed XIAO ESP32-C3.
> 2. Replace UNO R4 WiFi networking with ESP32-compatible networking libraries/APIs.
> 3. Keep the HTTPS POST to `/api/device-ingest` and HTTPS GET to `/api/device-control/{DEVICE_ID}` working.
> 4. Preserve the existing JSON payloads and overall control flow/behavior as much as possible.
> 5. Update BLE handling to something appropriate for ESP32-C3. If ArduinoBLE is not suitable on this platform, use an ESP32-compatible BLE library or gracefully disable BLE while keeping Wi-Fi functionality working. Prefer a practical working adaptation over strict preservation of the exact BLE implementation.
> 6. Change the pin mapping to the user's requested default mapping for XIAO ESP32-C3:
>    - S0 = D0
>    - S1 = D1
>    - S2 = D2
>    - S3 = D3
>    - OUT = D4
>    - LED = D5
>    Use the appropriate GPIO numbers or board pin constants for the XIAO ESP32-C3 Arduino environment.
> 7. Update comments/header in the sketch to reflect XIAO ESP32-C3 instead of UNO R4 WiFi.
> 8. Keep the rest of the sensor logic intact unless changes are required for compatibility.
> 9. If helpful, add brief compatibility comments for required board package/library expectations.
> 
> Deliverable:
> - Create a pull request with the code changes in `thecodingmastero/PlaqueTracker_App` targeting `main`.
> - Keep the PR focused on this sketch conversion only.
> 
> The following is the prior conversation context from the user's chat exploration (may be truncated):
> 
> User: make this code for a XIAO ESP32-C3
> 
> Assistant: Yes — but I need the target handled as a code change in your repo.
> 
> If you want, I can convert `tools/uno_r4_wifi_tcs3200_http.ino` for a **Seeed XIAO ESP32-C3** and open a PR in `thecodingmastero/PlaqueTracker_App`.
> 
> Two quick notes first:
> - The current sketch uses `WiFiS3.h`, which is for the **UNO R4 WiFi** stack and will need to be replaced on ESP32-C3.
> - Pin mappings will also need to change, because the XIAO ESP32-C3 has a different pinout and fewer exposed pins.
> 
> I need 2 things from you:
> 1. **Do you want me to open a PR** in `thecodingmastero/PlaqueTracker_App`?
> 2. What pin mapping should I use for the TCS3200 on the XIAO ESP32-C3? If you want, I can use a reasonable default like:
>    - `S0 = D0`
>    - `S1 = D1`
>    - `S2 = D2`
>    - `S3 = D3`
>    - `OUT = D4`
>    - `LED = D5`
> 
> If you want, reply with:
> - **“Yes, make the PR with default pins”**
> or
> - your custom pin mapping.
> 
> User: default
> 
> Assistant: I’ve started the PR creation flow for the XIAO ESP32-C3 conversion and I’m waiting for your confirmation in the GitHub dialog before I can continue.
> 
> 


</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

*This pull request was created from Copilot chat.*
>
